description_long: This is a traditional Japanese-style pagoda with a striking gold exterior and a multi-tiered roof structure. The building features two prominent stories, each with wide, gently upturned eaves and ornate railings, giving it a majestic and ceremonial appearance. The roof is dark, likely tiled, and contrasts sharply with the gold walls and balcony details. The base is elevated on a dark platform with visible support beams, and there are entryways and steps leading up to the main level. The structure is symmetrical, with decorative elements such as railings and possibly a small finial at the roof's apex. The overall design is reminiscent of the Kinkaku-ji (Golden Pavilion) in Kyoto, blending elegance with traditional Japanese architectural motifs.
description: A gold Japanese-style pagoda with two stories, dark upturned roofs, ornate railings, and an elevated platform base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, gold facade, and wide, upturned eaves with a dark platform and steps.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the layered gold balconies, dark tiled roof, and the building's depth and symmetry.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the gold and dark color contrast, with the platform and roof structure clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the gold balcony, upturned eaves, and the rear platform extension.
